Materials EngineeringAjay Gautam

### **Ajay Gautam**

Assistant Professor

#### Brief Info

Dr. Ajay Gautam is an Assistant Professor in Materials Engineering at IIT Jammu. His research focuses on the structure–transport relationships in materials for lithium-ion and solid-state batteries, advanced materials processing, and characterization using X-ray and neutron diffraction. Previously, he was an R&D Scientist at Samsung SDI Europe GmbH, Germany, where he focused on the processing of battery materials for next-generation battery technologies. Dr. Gautam earned his Ph.D. at the University of Münster and Justus Liebig University Giessen, Germany, under the supervision of Prof. Wolfgang Zeier. His doctoral work established key parameters of structural disorder and ion kinetics to unravel the structure–ion transport correlation in solid electrolytes. He pursued postdoctoral research with Prof. Marnix Wagemaker at Technical University of Delft, the Netherlands. There, he worked on the development of solid electrolytes, investigated their structure–transport properties using neutron diffraction, and explored silicon anode–solid electrolyte systems with a focus on degradation mechanisms and scaling strategies toward industrial relevance. He completed his B.Sc. (Research) and M.Sc. in Materials Science at the Indian Institute of Science (IISc), Bangalore, where he explored carbon nanotubes with transition metals for electrochemical and gas sensing applications.

#### Personal Website

Research group

#### Research Interest

1. Development of solid electrolytes
2. Solid-state batteries
3. Structure–transport correlations in energy storage materials
4. Materials characterization (X-ray and neutron diffraction)
5. Battery materials processing
6. Carbon-based materials for gas sensing, energy conversion, and storage applications.

### **Sarita**

Assistant Professor

#### Brief Info

Dr. Sarita works in the field of computational and experimental materials research, with a primary focus on phase transformations and microstructural evolution in different alloy systems. Her research combines density functional theory (DFT), atom probe tomography (APT), and small-angle scattering techniques (SAXS/SANS) to understand structure–property relationships at the atomic and nanoscale levels. She has extensive research experience in structural and nuclear materials, particularly in understanding microstructural evolution under extreme conditions such as high temperatures and radiation exposure. She also has a strong interest in the design of rare-earth-free magnetic materials, where microstructural tuning can be utilized to achieve desired magnetic properties.

1. Microstructural Evolution under Extreme Conditions
2. Phase Transformations in Materials
3. Structure–Property Correlation in Structural and Nuclear Materials
4. Rare-Earth-Free Magnetic Materials

#### Brief Info

Dr. Sri Tapaswi Nori has been serving as an Assistant Professor in the Department of Materials and Metallurgical Engineering at IIT Jammu since April 2026. His research is centered on understanding irradiation-induced effects and developing advanced alloys for nuclear energy applications. Dr. Nori utilizes a range of characterization techniques, including scanning and transmission electron microscopy, synchrotron X-ray experiments, and nanoindentation, to investigate the microstructural and mechanical behavior of alloys before and after neutron or ion irradiation. ### **Suman Sarkar**

Assistant Professor

#### Brief Info

Hello, I am Suman. I am a microscopist and experimental materials scientist who basically deals with microstructure-property correlation of materials. My research area focuses on the evolution of novel microstructure through fine tuning the thermodynamic properties and its effects on bulk properties. I extensively use Transmission Electron Microscope (TEM) for microstructural characterization. I use non-equilibrium rapid solidification processing techniques for varying the thermodynamic properties of materials and mesoscale modeling technique (Phase-Field Simulation) for studying the different physical attributes during microstructural evolution and phase transformation. I usually correlate mechanical and transport properties of materials with the evolved microstructure. You can visit the personal web page for more details.

### **Rajkumar V**

Assistant Professor

#### Brief Info

**Rajkumar Velu**, PhD, is an Assistant Professor, Department of Mechanical Engineering and Dual Affilation: Department of Materials Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology Jammu and worked as Visiting Assistant Professor at NTUST Taiwan in 2024. Priorly worked as a visiting research associate (2023) and Research Fellow at the Centre for Laser Aided Intelligent Manufacturing (CLAIM), University of Michigan, USA, and as a Post-Doctoral Research Fellow at the Digital Manufacturing and Design Centre (DManD), Singapore University of Technology and Design, Singapore. He completed his PhD thesis at the Auckland University of Technology, New Zealand, in selective laser sintering of specific biopolymer composites for biomedical application. Dr Velu is also the recipient of the prestigious GATE scholarship, best all-rounder award, best outgoing student gold medal for his academic performances at the university level. Consequently, he has considerable insights and knowledge on different polymer, hydrogels and composite materials used explicitly for the 3D printing process. He also has substantial experience in material and component characterization targeting specific responses, evaluating the complex thermo-mechanical aspects of powder bed processing, extrusion processing, and post-mortem studies for mechanical, electrical and biological attributes. More than 30 publications were generated from his research activities, and he received a scientific research award at an international conference conducted by Philadelphia University, Amman, Jordan 2010. Also, a recipient of the “young scientist award” 2018 at the Annual Conference on 3D printing and bioprinting in Health care, Singapore. Dr Velu’s research interests encompass engineering design, optimization, and developing advanced manufacturing processes (Additive Manufacturing) and materials for designing and fabrication for fuel cell, biomedical and aerospace applications. Moreover, based on his current research practice, he is involved in developing Artificial Intelligence into an additive manufacturing process to fabricate end-use products and eliminate post-mortem analysis.

#### Research Interest

Dr. Rajkumar Velu’s research is centered on additive manufacturing (AM) and advanced materials engineering, with a particular emphasis on the development, processing, and application of polymeric, metallic, and composite materials for high-performance and functional components. His multidisciplinary research integrates experimental and computational approaches to explore the following key areas:

1. Selective laser sintering of reactive polymers
2. Additive manufacturing of functionally graded structures
3. Fabrication of multifunctional components using continuous carbon fibre 3D printing
4. 3D bioprinting for biomedical and energy applications
5. SLM and Direct energy deposition of super alloys for aerospace applications

### Message

Materials are ubiquitous, thus playing a cardinal role in enabling most engineering applications. The famous quote by Richard Feynman "there is plenty of room at the bottom", is also apt for Materials Engineers, who strive to engineer the atomic-level structure of materials, in order to obtain desired properties. We, at IIT Jammu, are a young and vibrant team, actively involved in developing R&D programs and teaching exciting topics in Materials, aimed at both undergraduate students and Ph.D. scholars. The Department has made some not insignificant strides in just over four years of its existence, by having faculty covering a range of research & teaching areas ranging from metals to polymers. In the upcoming years, we look forward to interacting with bright young minds, and making a positive impact on education as well as helping Industry and Society at large.
